remember: Students will be able to define biodiversity and its importance.
Students will be able to recall the different types of biodiversity.
Students will be able to list the various levels of biodiversity.
understand: Students will be able to explain the concept of species diversity and its measurement.
Students will be able to describe the role of hotspots in conservation.
Students will be able to understand the significance of sacred groves in biodiversity conservation.
apply: Students will be able to apply the concept of biodiversity to real-life scenarios.
Students will be able to suggest measures for conservation of biodiversity.
Students will be able to analyze the impact of human activities on biodiversity.
analyze: Students will be able to analyze the relationships between different components of biodiversity.
Students will be able to evaluate the effectiveness of conservation strategies.
Students will be able to compare the biodiversity of different ecosystems.

ncert_objectives: As per NCERT objective 1: to understand the importance of biodiversity and its conservation.
As per NCERT objective 2: to analyze the impact of human activities on biodiversity.
As per NCERT objective 3: to learn about the different strategies for conservation of biodiversity.
board_exam_objectives: What CBSE tests from this chapter: the ability to define and explain key terms related to biodiversity and conservation.
What CBSE tests from this chapter: the ability to analyze case studies and suggest measures for conservation of biodiversity.
What CBSE tests from this chapter: the ability to evaluate the importance of biodiversity and its conservation.
